mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which compress corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rate above 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal tightening monetary policy that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 215 and FEDFUNDS > 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) indicates recessionary pressures that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening supply-side activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where both INDPRO < 110 and HOUST < 900k.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in consumer spending (RSAFS) above 375 billion signals strong demand conditions that boost corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS > 375 billion.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 110.
